data.title: Tesla Cashes in $80M Revenue From Its Bitcoin Holdings In Q3
data.description:
Tesla Gains $80M on Bitcoin in Q3, Keeps 11,509 BTC Untouched Amid Market Rally

Tesla’s Bitcoin bet continues to pay off. The electric vehicle giant recorded an $80 million gain on its Bitcoin holdings in Q3 2025 — its largest crypto revaluation boost since 2021 — while maintaining its long-held position of 11,509 BTC.

BREAKING:

BITCOIN JUST HIT A NEW ALL-TIME HIGH

Tesla owns 11,509 bitcoins, worth currently $1.4 billion$TSLApic.twitter.com/GwCxFbZERa

— Dalton Brewer (@daltybrewer) October 5, 2025

The company’s third-quarter earnings report, published on Oct. 22, showed that its Bitcoin stash, valued at $1.31 billion, climbed from $1.23 billion in Q2. The increase came solely from Bitcoin’s price appreciation, as Tesla made no new purchases or sales during the period.

Tesla’s digital asset holdings have remained unchanged since early 2022, but their paper value has risen sharply alongside Bitcoin’s resurgence in 2025.

Tesla began applying fair-value accounting earlier this year, a change that requires digital assets to be reflected at current market prices rather than their historical purchase cost. The adjustment allowed the automaker to recognize an $80 million unrealized gain under “other income,” marking its strongest Bitcoin-related quarter in four years.

The updated reporting standard has helped clarify the real-time impact of Bitcoin’s price movements on Tesla’s balance sheet, a transparency shift welcomed by investors tracking corporate crypto exposure.

With its 11,509 BTC, Tesla now ranks as the 11th largest corporate Bitcoin holder globally — sitting behind MicroStrategy, Galaxy Digital, and Block, but ahead of mining firms like Hut 8.

This also marks Tesla’s second consecutive quarter of Bitcoin gains, mirroring the broader digital asset market recovery throughout 2025.

Profits Fall Despite Crypto Boost

Tesla reported total Q3 revenue of $25.18 billion, a 6% increase year-over-year, with automotive sales contributing $21.2 billion. However, net income dropped 37% to $1.37 billion, or $0.39 per share, compared to $2.17 billion, or $0.62 per share, in the same period last year.

The company attributed the decline in profitability to lower vehicle prices and rising operational costs, which surged more than 50% amid major AI and R&D investments.

While Bitcoin gains contributed to other income, they were excluded from Tesla’s adjusted earnings per share, aligning with standard accounting treatment for non-operational assets.

Tesla first entered the crypto market in February 2021, when it purchased $1.5 billion in Bitcoin, becoming one of the first major U.S. corporations to hold the asset on its balance sheet. It briefly sold a small portion later that year to “test liquidity,” before reclassifying Bitcoin as a strategic treasury asset.

Despite significant market volatility and broader crypto downturns, Tesla has held firm through each cycle — signaling long-term conviction in Bitcoin as what it calls a “liquid alternative to cash.”

Meanwhile, blockchain data from Arkham Intelligence revealed that a wallet associated with SpaceX, another Elon Musk-led company, recently moved about $268 million in Bitcoin to two new addresses. Analysts described the transfers as internal fund management rather than asset liquidation.

SpaceX moved $268M in Bitcoin earlier — its first major $BTC transfer in three months. - Arkham pic.twitter.com/hwM7rdhrMb

— Sjuul | AltCryptoGems (@AltCryptoGems) October 21, 2025

Tesla’s steady hand in holding Bitcoin through turbulent cycles — now rewarded with its biggest revaluation gain in years — underscores a broader corporate trend: digital assets are gradually becoming a normalized component of balance sheets, even for global blue-chip firms navigating economic headwinds.

data.title: The man with the LUNΑ tattoo earned 500 million in a single season to "avenge" his shame
data.description:
Author: Seedly.eth

In 2022, Michael Novogratz got a LUNA tattoo on his arm. At the time, he was one of the most active evangelists in the crypto industry, managing the asset management platform Galaxy Digital, which was riding the crest of the previous crypto bull market.

However, in May 2022, LUNA plummeted to zero within a week, wiping out $60 billion in market capitalization. This event, known as a "death spiral," nearly destroyed the entire Terra ecosystem and thrust Galaxy into the spotlight. For most companies, such an incident would have been career-ending. But Galaxy didn't give up. Three years later, it made a surprising comeback—a net profit of $505 million, a stock price surge of nearly 9% in a single day, and a market capitalization back to $16.5 billion. This time, it wasn't just a comeback; it was the results that spoke for themselves. Reflection, starting with a letter. When LUNA collapsed, most companies chose to remain silent. But Novogratz chose to sit down and write a letter. In it, he admitted to misjudging the project's risks and reflected on the prudence that investment institutions should exercise. He wrote that the tattoo would not be covered up, but would become a permanent reminder: "Venture capital requires humility."

This was the beginning of Galaxy's transformation. Since then, the company has gradually reduced its reliance on "short-term market trends," reorganized its core capabilities, and attempted to win market trust in a more sustainable way.

Profit “Avenge”

In the third quarter of 2025, Galaxy Digital delivered the most impressive financial report in its seven years of establishment:

  • Net profit: US$505 million (a 16-fold increase year-on-year)

  • Adjusted gross profit: US$728 million (a 143% increase month-on-month)

  • Trading revenue: US$295 million (annualized to US$1.2 billion)

  • Asset management scale: exceeded US$9 billion

  • Core institutional client treasury asset injection: US$4.5 billion

  • Once completed, Helios could generate $1 billion in annual revenue, rivaling its trading business in terms of revenue volume, becoming the company's "third pillar."

    Key Financial Report Data at a Glance (Q3 2025)

    The team behind it all has a Wall Street background

    Galaxy’s turnaround is inseparable from its star-level leadership team – this core team, known in the industry as the “Galaxy Fleet,” perfectly blends the steadiness of Wall Street veterans with the sharpness of crypto pioneers:

    It is no longer just a mark of failure, but more like a medal, reminding everyone who moves forward in the industry:

    Only those who have gone through the trough and dared to leave a mark are truly worthy of the highlight moment.

data.title: World Cutest Bunni DEX DeFi Shuts Down After $8.4 Hack, Distributes Remaining Funds To Holders
data.description:
Bunni DEX Shuts Down After $8.4M Exploit, Highlighting the Growing Toll of DeFi Hacks

The world's cutest decentralized exchange, Bunni DEX, is facing a harsh reality, after announcing that it will be shutting down operations following an $8.4 million exploit in September, becoming the second DeFi project this week to fold under the pressure of mounting security breaches and financial strain.

In a post on X on Thursday, the Bunni team described how the project has been facing tremendous financial pressure after the hack, citing the enormous costs associated with auditing, monitoring, and relaunching the protocol after the exploit.

“The recent exploit has forced Bunni’s growth to a halt, and in order to securely relaunch, we’d need to pay six to seven figures in audit and monitoring expenses alone — requiring capital that we simply don’t have.”

Hello everyone, it is with saddened hearts that we announce the shutdown of Bunni.

The recent exploit has forced Bunni's growth to a halt, and in order to securely relaunch we'd need to pay 6-7 figures in audit & monitoring expenses alone – requiring capital that we simply don't…

— Bunni (@bunni_xyz) October 23, 2025

The announcement marks a somber end for one of DeFi’s most promising liquidity platforms, which had grown rapidly in 2025. According to DefiLlama, Bunni’s total value locked (TVL) surged from just $2.23 million in June to nearly $80 million by August, before the exploit halted its rise.

The Attack That Halted Bunni’s Momentum

The September 2 exploit drained $8.4 million from Bunni’s liquidity pools across Ethereum and Layer-2 network Unichain, forcing the team to halt operations. A subsequent investigation found that malicious actors exploited vulnerabilities in Bunni’s custom codebase.

Built atop Uniswap v4, Bunni was designed to enhance liquidity efficiency through its proprietary Liquidity Distribution Function (LDF) — a mechanism intended to automatically optimize yield for liquidity providers.

🚨 #BunniDEX loses $2.4M in #stablecoins! Users urged to withdraw fast! Who #hacked the #liquidity? #CryptoHack#BunniDEX#Ethereum#DeFi#BlockchainSecurity
>> https://t.co/SyJgy1WuXdpic.twitter.com/hP316dWIk1

— Altcoins Talks (@AltcoinsTalks) September 2, 2025

However, this complexity ultimately became the cause of its demise as the attackers have reportedly manipulated the LDF curve to trigger faulty rebalancing logic, ultimately draining the protocol without setting off security alerts.

Following the incident, Bunni offered the attacker a 10% white-hat bounty to return the stolen funds. But these efforts were to no avail as these efforts were only met with radio silence from the hackers.

Bunni's Final Act Of Defiance

Despite shutting down, Bunni’s final act has been met with praise across the developer community. In what the team described as an effort to “let innovation live on,” they have relicensed the Bunni v2 smart contracts from a Business Source License to an MIT open-source license, allowing developers to freely build upon its codebase.

Bunni DEX re-licenses its v2 smart contracts under MIT, encouraging developer use. It's the second crypto project to shut this week, earning kudos for openness. pic.twitter.com/Pe1aJhFcwh

— Bitcoin Dominance (@MoneyHustl41075) October 23, 2025

This move effectively turns Bunni’s innovations — such as its liquidity distribution functions, surge fees, and autonomous rebalancing mechanisms — into open resources for the wider DeFi ecosystem.

The team has also called users to withdraw their remaining assets from the platform while they still can. The remaining treasury assets will be distributed to BUNNI, LIT, and veBUNNI tokenholders once legal approvals are complete. Notably, team members will not receive any compensation from the remaining funds.

The wait is over.

Bunni v2 is LIVE on Ethereum Mainnet, @Base, and @Arbitrum! 🐰🥕

As the first DEX built on top of Uniswap v4, Bunni v2 offers programmable liquidity features that help LPs build yield-maximized, dynamic, and automated liquidity pools. Our rehypothecation hook… pic.twitter.com/OjkSaa7wLf

— Bunni (@bunni_xyz) February 1, 2025

For now, Bunni said it continues to work with law enforcement to recover the stolen $8.4 million.

DeFi Faces Another Blow

Bunni’s closure follows closely on the heels of Layer-1 blockchain Kadena’s founding team, which also announced this week that it would cease operations amid harsh market conditions.

While Kadena’s network will continue as a community-led project, the twin announcements have cast a shadow over the DeFi and blockchain sector, where project shutdowns are increasingly tied to security exploits and funding shortfalls.

These back-to-back closures highlight the deepening fragility of the DeFi landscape. Despite billions of dollars in total value locked across protocols, many teams remain under-resourced to defend against sophisticated attacks — a problem compounded by the rising cost of audits and the complexity of custom smart contract designs.

For Bunni, the end of operations might not spell the end of its impact. By open-sourcing its code, the project leaves behind tools that may yet strengthen the ecosystem it once served — even as its own fall stands as a stark reminder of the risks that continue to plague decentralized finance.

data.title: From the cryptocurrency casino to the era of super individuals
data.description:
Ever since I posted that tweet about wanting to become a "super individual," I've received a lot of good-natured ridicule from my friends. Some accused me of inventing a new word, playing with the concept of "super," while others joked that I might become a "super leek." But I'm serious. I've spent a lot of time thinking about what a "super individual" is. Drawing on my own experience, I've established a preliminary framework for how to become one from the cryptocurrency world. I've been dabbling in the cryptocurrency world for nearly eight years. While I haven't achieved significant results, I've also gained a lot. As one of the lucky few, I sincerely hope you'll learn about this concept sooner rather than later. I also hope that through this article, I can connect with like-minded friends and grow together.

In addition, I wrote a relatively naive post last week, which basically explained: Super Individual & Cryptocurrency Trading Life; I used the absurd example of "Nine Generations of Leeks" to explain it, which laid a preliminary ideological foundation. The idea was not very perfect at the time, but it was good at sharing the joy of enlightenment at that moment. If you are interested, you can take a look.

1. What is a "Super Individual"?

Super Individual = Super Individual

Super Individual is closest to the original meaning, emphasizing "individual awakening and growth", not just making money or starting a business, which can also be extended to three dimensions:

  1. Sovereign Individual Self-sovereignty

  2. Solo entrepreneur   Independent entrepreneurship

  3. Self-Evolving Individual Self-growth

In this era of AI + Crypto, the so-called "Super Individual" is simply a person who, in a world dominated by algorithms and capital, can simultaneously master AI productivity and financial sovereignty, making his cognition, wealth, and influence independent. If we're truly headed towards a cyberpunk world, a life "fed by AI," "driven by algorithms," "driven by market sentiment," and "manipulated by insiders" can be quite suffocating. I want to be that kind of ordinary person—one with my own computing power, sovereignty, awareness, and the ability to grow. I don't necessarily make the most money, but I can control my own pace of life, unconstrained by most rules and regulations. Don't think only super-powered individuals like Musk can escape the mold. True freedom, in fact, has little to do with money or status; it's a mindset and an attitude. Confucius said that at seventy, one's mindset can "follow one's heart's desire without transgressing the rules." I believe that in this era, even if we're not the "chosen one" to make a fortune, this generation of crypto natives has every chance of achieving this in their twenties and thirties.

Second, why does this concept only hold true in the AI ​​+ Crypto era?

In the Web2 era, people were fed by algorithms, kidnapped by traffic, exploited by data, and coerced by values. In the Web3 + AI era, for the first time, we have two readily available tools and powers:

1/ The first weapon: AI = Amplify your cognition, creativity, and productivity

Equipping individuals with the power of a "thousand-person think tank," lowering knowledge barriers and creating "cognitive compounding." Someone who knows how to use Vibe Coding and is willing to use AI to improve their productivity can be as productive as a company before. 2/ The Second Weapon: Crypto = Amplifying Your Wealth and Financial Sovereignty. A single wallet allows individuals to, for the first time, possess absolute sovereignty over their assets, freeing them from dependence on banks, institutions, or intermediaries. The combination of these two is the starting point for becoming a "superindividual." AI empowers productivity, while crypto empowers financial sovereignty. If you can learn independently, earn independently, and express yourself freely, unfettered by algorithms, institutions, or emotions, then I believe you're not far from becoming a "superindividual." As for the path forward, I'm still exploring. 3. Starting from the Crypto World: Five Stages of Becoming a Superindividual. Our generation is unique. Thanks to cryptocurrency, we have more free time, accumulated wealth, and developed our own unique ways of making a living. Some rely on cryptocurrency trading, some on airdrops, some on research, and some on content creation. To put it bluntly, everyone who survives is already closer to awakening than those who remained in the casino in the previous round. However, the most difficult aspect of the cryptocurrency world isn't "survival" but "living with understanding." As the author, I still struggle in the cryptocurrency world today, and often worry that I'm not the chosen one to make big money and achieve great results. However, I've come to terms with this idea and aspire to become a super individual. Perhaps the following framework can inspire you: Stage One: Awakening — From Digital Anxiety to Mental Clarity Recognize the limitations of "cryptocurrency trading life." We believe the market's ultimate goal is financial freedom, but that's actually an illusion. The sharp rise and fall of cryptocurrency prices are merely the illusion of a bull market. The real crux of the matter lies in whether you can achieve your desired expectations and maintain your composure. Cultivation Direction: 1. Stop defining the value of a day by gains and losses. 2. Start introspecting and observing your desires (what you truly want) and fears (what you truly fear). Heart Method: Find a rational anchor between not being greedy during rapid growth and not panicking during plummeting prices. The sign of awakening is that you no longer just watch the market, but begin to focus on your inner self. Stage Two: Balance — Integrating Investing into Your Life. When one day you make a fortune and don't feel ecstatic, or a loss isn't as devastating as you imagined, you'll realize that investment gains and losses are just one dimension of life. True growth comes from balance: thinking and spiritual practice, AI and humanity, wealth and health, solitude and social interaction all require balance. The market is just one of the training grounds for achieving this balanced middle path. Cultivation Directions: 1/ Reserve 40% of your energy daily for your "non-market self." 2/ Let creativity, reading, and exercise become effective remedies for anxiety. Stage 3: Autonomy — Rebuilding Your Value System The biggest illusion in the cryptocurrency world is the illusion that "others know the answers" through comparison. True autonomy involves comparing yourself only to your past self and redefining your own belief system. Cultivation Directions: 1/ Don't be swayed by giant exchanges, KOLs, coin issuers, or social media buzz. 2/ Build your own "mental research model" and "asset allocation system." 3/ Extending wealth sovereignty into time sovereignty and spiritual sovereignty. Stage 4: Long-Term Compounding and Self-Sovereignty — Growth is Faith Compounding is not just about the growth of your assets; it's also about the multi-dimensional accumulation of cognition, personality, skills, influence, and spiritual strength. The market fluctuates unpredictably, but only time compounds for those who are clear-headed. When you begin to think in terms of extended timeframes, such as decades, you'll become more confident and at ease in the market. Meanwhile, sovereignty is the core keyword of this stage. It embodies a sense of control. It means not only controlling your assets, keys, and identity, but also—controlling your emotions, choices, attention, and the rhythm of your destiny. Cultivation Direction: Design a ten-year life blueprint: focusing on five dimensions: wealth, skills, work, connections, and spirit. Let AI become your cognitive lever: learn faster, express more effectively, and gain deeper insights. Let crypto become your capital lever: understand the underlying logic of ownership, liquidity, and value transfer. Build your own Life Operating System (Life OS): encompassing five modules: mind management, information intake, learning cycles, financial review, and creative output. Heart Method: True freedom isn't about doing whatever you want, but about not being forced to do anything. When you possess sovereignty, you no longer rely on external stimuli to confirm your existence, because you have become the "root node" of your own system.

Stage 5: Infinite Possibilities and Infinite Games

At this point, your goal is no longer to "make money" or "win," but to enter the state of the Infinite Game.

In finite games, people pursue victory or defeat;

In infinite games, people pursue continuity and creation.

From the perspective of the cryptocurrency market, you begin to understand that the market is not a casino, but an energy amplifier. It allows you to transcend cycles while continuously opening up infinite possibilities, using the laws of the market to nourish more dimensions of life.

In investing: Build stability as the foundation, and use small investments to achieve big gains. You no longer chase dreams of getting rich quick, nor do you easily go all in. Instead, establish your own "long-term stable allocation + small, high-risk attempts" mechanism:

  • Major positions are used for steady compounding—stablecoins and BTC are all dividends given by the times

  • Small positions are reserved for pursuing excess returns and high-risk exploration

  • Afford to lose, but also enjoy making money; both winning and losing can bring learning and inspiration.

In life: Let the market feed back into your life. When you are no longer held hostage by money, money will begin to serve you. Possessing any of the following mindsets can be very helpful:

  • Making money isn't the goal; it's about buying back time and freedom;

  • Investment returns aren't just numbers; they give you the time to read, travel, exercise, and create;

  • The setbacks and fluctuations the market brings you become the truest psychological training camp.

    • Training Direction:

    • Build your own asset portfolio and cash flow system: prioritizing stability and supplemented by trial and error.

    • Let making money become the fuel that drives the quality of your life, not a source of stress.

    • Let losses become feedback, not punishment.

    • Use market cycles as the rhythm of life, and use the compound interest of wealth to drive the compound interest of time, health, and knowledge.

    Key Point: A true master isn't someone who exits the market, but someone who continues to evolve their life amidst its fluctuations. The end of the infinite game isn't "winning," but continuous upgrading, continued existence, continuous creation, and continuous growth.

    Summary

    Finally, I want to say to the ordinary people still struggling in the cryptocurrency world (myself included): When it comes to money, don't be that loser who earns a lot but still feels empty, anxious, and pretentious. A "super individual" might be a life attitude that even the wealthy individuals of the past few decades would envy.

    Toward this goal, wealth may simply be a byproduct of the energy you emit. Your true product is a self-driven life system. This preliminary framework is actually an evolutionary chain from cryptocurrency trader → investor → long-term compounder → infinite player → superindividual. While there are still many imperfections, I plan to establish a small, long-term "superindividual" group soon, relying solely on member referrals. I hope to gradually transform from "cryptocurrency friends" in the cryptocurrency world into true lifelong friends. I hope to gradually transform from "cryptocurrency trading life" in the cryptocurrency world into a "superindividual" in control of my own self. Perhaps this is just a bit of utopian fantasy, a moment of clarity. Will one wrong move lead to a complete loss? Will I still be trapped in the bitter sea of ​​trading? Will I still be close to my upside-down dreams? I don't know. But I truly look forward to this path to becoming a superindividual.

    The road ahead is long and arduous, but if you can encourage and grow with like-minded people, your chances of success may be higher!

data.title: SEAL Leads Crypto’s New Defense Alliance: MetaMask, Phantom Unite Against Phishing Epidemic
data.description:
SEAL Leads Web3’s ‘Decentralized Immune System’ as MetaMask and Phantom Join Global Anti-Phishing Alliance

As phishing scams drain over $400 million in crypto this year alone, a powerful new alliance led by the Security Alliance (SEAL) is rising to fight back.

In a landmark collaboration, MetaMask, Phantom, WalletConnect, and Backpack have joined SEAL to launch a global phishing defense network — a real-time, decentralized security system designed to detect and neutralize threats before they spread across the crypto ecosystem.

Described as the industry’s first “decentralized immune system,” SEAL’s new network enables verified phishing intelligence to move instantly across participating wallets.

We’ve joined forces to launch a global phishing defense network that can protect more users across the entire ecosystem.

Security gets stronger when we work together. 🤝 https://t.co/Ah8RuiTQ6D

— MetaMask.eth 🦊 (@MetaMask) October 21, 2025

The mechanism works through SEAL’s Verifiable Phishing Reports, a system that allows users and researchers worldwide to submit evidence of phishing sites. Once verified, these reports are automatically distributed to all connected wallets, helping them block malicious domains in real time. SEAL explained in a statement.

“This means quicker response times to new phishing threats and more funds saved.”

By linking leading wallets under one shared defense protocol, SEAL’s network creates a continuous feedback loop — where every verified report strengthens the entire ecosystem’s resilience against attackers.

MetaMask and Phantom Rally Behind SEAL’s Initiative

For the first time, wallet competitors are uniting under a single, decentralized security standard. “Drainers are a constant cat-and-mouse game,” said Ohm Shah, a security researcher at MetaMask. “By working with SEAL, we can finally move faster and throw a wrench into the drainer’s infrastructure.”

SEAL’s collaborative model ensures that intelligence moves faster than attackers. Once a phishing site is identified, all participating wallets — including MetaMask, Phantom, WalletConnect, and Backpack — automatically receive the alert, allowing users to avoid scams before damage occurs.

The initiative’s ultimate goal, according to SEAL, is to extend participation to as many wallet and dApp providers as possible, creating a unified security backbone for Web3.

The timing couldn’t be more urgent. According to CertiK, phishing attacks remain the leading cause of crypto-related losses in 2025, accounting for more than $400 million in stolen funds in just the first half of the year.

Crypto scams in 2025 are on another level.

You’ll see promises like:

🚀 “40% returns every week”
🤖 “AI bot does the trading for you”

But behind the hype? Just traps. Here’s how to stay safe 🧵 pic.twitter.com/4Q22Zdke1R

— GoMining (@GoMining_token) September 11, 2025

Unlike traditional hacks that exploit smart contract bugs, phishing scams rely on deception — tricking users into approving malicious transactions or connecting to fake dApps. These “drainer” schemes evolve quickly, often rotating domains, migrating to offshore servers, and using cloaking tools to bypass blocklists.

Beyond phishing, overall crypto-related hacks totaled $127 million in September, down from $163 million in August, according to data from PeckShield. The month saw nearly 20 major exploits, heavily concentrated in a few large-scale incidents.

Among the most significant were UXLINK, which lost $44 million after its multisig wallet was compromised — leading to the unauthorized minting of 10 trillion tokens and a 90% price crash — and SwissBorg, which suffered a $41.5 million breach through its Solana API partner, Kiln. Both projects are currently working with exchanges, law enforcement, and white-hat hackers to recover funds and compensate affected users.

🚨 LATEST: The hacker behind the UXLINK attack was hit by a phishing scam, losing 542M $UXLINK worth $48M. pic.twitter.com/U7plHTS5BI

— Cointelegraph (@Cointelegraph) September 23, 2025

Despite the decline in overall hack volume, industry experts say phishing remains the most pervasive and preventable form of crypto theft — one that SEAL’s decentralized immune system is specifically designed to combat.

Building Trust Through Collaboration

By positioning itself at the center of this global defense initiative, SEAL is redefining how security intelligence is shared in Web3. Instead of relying on centralized authorities or slow manual reporting, SEAL’s protocol transforms security into a community-driven, real-time response system.

This approach could mark a turning point for crypto users who’ve long faced phishing scams as an unavoidable risk. SEAL said in a statement

“Security in Web3 can’t be siloed anymore. The only way to fight decentralized threats is through decentralized defense.”

With MetaMask, Phantom, and other major players backing SEAL’s mission, the industry may finally have found its most powerful weapon yet — a decentralized immune system built by the community, for the community.

data.title: If Bitcoin isn't a "cryptocurrency," how is it different?
data.description:
Author: Bradley Peak, Source: Cointelegraph, Compiled by Shaw Jinse Finance

1. Bitcoin is not a "cryptocurrency"

A tweet from Jack Dorsey has reignited the old question: Is Bitcoin part of the "cryptocurrency" category, or a distinct category unto itself?

On October 19, 2025, Jack Dorsey posted three words on Platform X: "Bitcoin is not a cryptocurrency." The post quickly gained traction on the platform and in media reports. This reflected his long-held view that Bitcoin should be considered a currency with its own rules and history, and should not be lumped into the broader token market.

Dorsey believes that Bitcoin should be considered a separate category. Initially, it had no foundation, no pre-mine, and relatively conservative governance. The Bitcoin network was designed primarily for payments and savings, unlike the rapidly evolving smart contract platforms and application tokens that serve a wide range of use cases.

Let's analyze this argument.

To understand why, it's necessary to understand how Bitcoin's design, governance, and regulation differ from the rest of the cryptocurrency world.

2. Monetary Policy and Issuance: Fixed Rules vs. Flexible Policies

Starting with supply, Bitcoin's issuance follows a fixed schedule, while most other networks treat supply as an adjustable function.

Bitcoin is issued as a block reward, which halves approximately every 210,000 blocks until the total supply reaches 21 million BTC. The fourth halving will occur in April 2024, at block height 840,000, reducing the reward from 6.25 BTC to 3.125 BTC. Each halving causes miners to rely more on transaction fees and less on newly issued Bitcoins. Changing Bitcoin's issuance requires overwhelming social consensus among users running nodes, allowing investors to predict supply years in advance. This predictability remains a core part of its appeal as a store of value. Most other networks consider monetary policy a design choice. For example, Ethereum: Ethereum Improvement Proposal (EIP) 1559 introduced a base fee burn mechanism to reduce net issuance during periods of high demand; a merged update migrated the network to Proof-of-Stake (PoS), reducing total issuance. Together, these changes create a supply model that dynamically adjusts based on network activity. This flexibility enhances user experience and enables new features, while Bitcoin's rigidity is designed to maintain its credibility as a currency. 3. Consensus and Security Budget: PoW Minimalism vs. PoS Upgrade Speed ​​How a blockchain ensures its own security will determine everything that follows. Bitcoin trades security for work, while PoS systems provide security through stake. On the Bitcoin network, miners use energy to add blocks, and full nodes enforce a small and conservative set of rules. Its scripting language is deliberately concise, not Turing-complete. Fewer components means less potential for failure, which is why changes to the base layer are extremely rare and strictly limited. As block rewards continue to halve, miners' income gradually shifts from issuing new coins to transaction fees—Bitcoin's long-term "security budget." This raises important questions about the future, such as how incentives will be maintained during periods of low fees. This also demonstrates that the surge in activity driving up transaction fees, as well as the stable use of layers like the Lightning Network, are crucial to the miner economy. Many crypto platforms, particularly Ethereum, use a proof-of-stake (PoS) mechanism. Validators lock up ETH, earning rewards for proposing and attesting blocks and potentially facing penalties for misconduct. This model allows for faster upgrades: the 2022 Merge upgrade switched to PoS, Shapella in 2023 allowed withdrawals, and EIP-4844 in 2024 reduced rollup data costs. While Bitcoin prioritizes the security, stability, and minimal changes to its underlying infrastructure, most PoS networks emphasize faster upgrades and higher throughput. 4. Governance and Culture: "Rigidity and Optimization" in Practice Who changes the rules, how quickly, and how can security be guaranteed? While Bitcoin was designed for slow evolution, application-focused blockchains prioritize speed and flexibility. Bitcoin was designed for slow evolution. Proposals initially emerge as Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs), are publicly debated, and only advance when there is broad support from developers, miners, and node operators. There are no on-chain votes or foundations to guide decision-making. Upgrades are typically rolled out as soft forks to maintain compatibility for older nodes. In 2021, the Taproot upgrade used a "rapid trial" signaling mechanism, reaching lock-in in June and activating on block 709,632 on November 14, 2021. This lengthy process gives developers, miners, and node operators time to coordinate and reduces activation risk. This cadence (few changes, lots of discussion) is what is often referred to as Bitcoin's "rigidity." Smart contract platforms take the opposite approach. Ethereum introduces changes through the EIP process and follows a steady release cycle—for example, merged withdrawals and prototype Danks sharding to reduce data costs.

Different goals, different cadences: Bitcoin maintains its credibility through conservative revisions, while application-focused blockchains focus on rolling out new features and maintaining developer activity.

5. Running on Top: Payment Applications vs. General-Purpose Applications

Bitcoin keeps its base layer lean: unspent transaction output (UTXO) accounting, limited stack-based scripting (intentionally not Turing-complete), and relatively simple other logic.

The majority of Bitcoin's payment activity is shifting to second-layer networks like the Lightning Network. These use bidirectional channels and hashed time-locked contracts (HTLCs) to enable instant, low-fee payments without changing the underlying rules. Everyday transactions take place off-chain, while settlement remains anchored to the main network. Smart contract platforms take the opposite approach. Ethereum supports rich, stateful contracts on its Layer 1 and encourages composability—decentralized finance (DeFi), non-fungible tokens (NFTs), and on-chain games layered on top of each other. This approach accelerates experimentation but relies on a flexible and regularly upgraded base layer. Bitcoin continues to explore the fringes. The introduction of Ordinals and Runes around the 2024 halving pushed fees to record highs, increased miner revenue, and provided a real-world test of fee-driven security. Crucially, none of this changed Bitcoin's monetary rules or minimalist underlying design. The model remains valid: maintain a stable foundation and allow new activities to develop on top of or alongside it. 6. Market Structure and Implications: Bitcoin's Separate Category Exchange-traded funds (ETFs), options, and fund flow data suggest that institutions are treating Bitcoin differently than other cryptocurrency markets. On January 10, 2024, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) approved a rule change allowing exchanges to list and trade spot Bitcoin exchange-traded products (ETPs). This decision brought Bitcoin to mainstream markets, including the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), Arca, Nasdaq, and the Chicago Board Options Exchange (Cboe). These are the very platforms used by brokerages, registered investment advisors (RIAs), and pension funds. Whatever you call the asset class, retirement and wealth management platforms now have a dedicated channel for Bitcoin. This expands market infrastructure. By the end of 2024, US regulators had approved spot Bitcoin ETF options, and the Chicago Board Options Exchange (Cboe) had launched index options tied to a basket of Bitcoin funds. In short, it leveraged tools already familiar to institutions for risk transfer and price discovery—something most tokens still lacked. Fund flow data clearly illustrates this shift. During 2024 and 2025, subscriptions and redemptions for new funds became routine, with dashboards tracking assets and net flows. Investors accessed Bitcoin through traditional channels, rather than through cryptocurrency-native platforms. Policy signals also pointed in the same direction. US derivatives regulators have long classified Bitcoin as a commodity. In 2025, staff from the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) noted that registered exchanges could facilitate trading in certain spot commodity-crypto products. Taken together, distribution channels, hedging tools, fund flow reports, and regulatory labels all provide strong evidence for Jack's argument that "Bitcoin is not a cryptocurrency." The market has already categorized it into different categories.

data.title: From a $4 billion valuation to closure, who will be next after Kadena's collapse?
data.description:
The Kadena Organization, the operating company behind the Kadena blockchain, announced its shutdown on October 21st in a formal, calm, yet painfully concise announcement. The company thanked the community, cited "market conditions" as the reason for the shutdown, and confirmed that it would immediately cease all business activities and blockchain maintenance. In a final announcement on the X platform (formerly Twitter), the team reminded users that the blockchain will continue to technically survive, as miners will continue to secure the network and the code will remain open source.

However, beneath this technical "survival" lies a harsher reality: Kadena's economic vitality and community foundation are no longer there.

Kadena's shutdown is not an isolated failure, but part of a deeper structural adjustment in the cryptocurrency industry.

In this process, those infrastructure layers that have never achieved "product-market fit" (PMF), never formed a professional positioning, and never developed attractive supporting applications will gradually exit the market.

The Road to a Desperate Situation

Kadena's starting point is both "industry background" and "grand vision".

The project was founded by former JPMorgan engineers Stuart Popejoy and William Martino. When it was launched in 2018, it promised to provide features that Ethereum could not achieve at the time, such as high-throughput Proof-of-Work (PoW) smart contracts through a system called "

" Its proprietary programming language, Pact, features "human-readable code" and "formal verification," aiming to position Kadena as a blockchain network that offers both security and scalability. However, "innovation without user adoption is ultimately an unfinished story." Kadena launched its mainnet in 2019, building a limited developer ecosystem. According to CoinMarketCap data, its valuation approached $4 billion in 2021, but has since plummeted by more than 99% from its peak. During this period, only a few mainstream decentralized applications such as Babena emerged in the Kadena ecosystem, and Babena's peak total value locked (TVL) was only US$8 million. At the same time, liquidity continued to flow to ecosystems with greater user appeal, first Ethereum and Solana, and then second-layer networks such as Base built directly on Ethereum. Cryptocurrency researcher Noveleader pointed out that for many years, Kadena has failed to shake the dominance of the Ethereum Virtual Machine, and the price trend of its token KDA and the development of projects within the ecosystem have also been difficult. This phenomenon reveals the core contradiction behind Kadena's suspension: in the current cryptocurrency economy, there is a serious mismatch between supply and demand. Since 2021, venture capital has injected billions of dollars into "modular first-layer networks," "second-layer networks," and "Rollups," all of which promise to solve problems of "scalability," "decentralization," or "transaction costs."

However,the actual user market size has barely grown.

According to data from L2Beat and DeFiLlama, there are currently over 100 rolling upgrade projects and over 200 independent chains running in various ecosystems (from Ethereum forks to Cosmos-based application chains).

But the vast majority of them have fewer than 2,000 daily active users (DAU). The reason is simple: they are all competing for the same participants, including traders, yield farmers and liquidity providers, but fail to provide any new value.

Startup developer Greg Tomaselli accurately summarized the situation:

“Blockchain networks without clear value propositions and broad application scenarios will ultimately fail.”

The illusion of differentiation

The collapse of Kadena reveals a truth that the industry is reluctant to face:

Technological novelty does not equal “product-market fit”.

Almost every new blockchain claims to solve the problems of "scalability," "latency," or "gas efficiency."

But few projects can clearly explain: when most users are deeply integrated into the Ethereum, Solana, or Binance ecosystem, who needs a new chain?

Like many "aspiring first-layer networks," Kadena attempts to differentiate itself through "performance metrics." Its chain architecture provides high throughput while maintaining the security of proof-of-work.

But in the cryptocurrency industry, "performance" has long been a "homogeneous commodity."

Once a network can process thousands of transactions per second, the core of "differentiation" shifts from "speed" to "purpose."

Ethereum's success doesn't stem from being "the fastest," but from becoming the "default ecosystem" for tokens, decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), and decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols.Solana's rise is due to its fostering of high-frequency trading and social application scenarios. Like projects like EOS, Kadena has never clearly defined its core positioning beyond being "better than existing chains." This "build the chain first, wait for the market later" logic is at the heart of the infrastructure bubble. Each new chain chases "imaginary demand," while users continue to flock to ecosystems with "liquidity and community culture." The end result: hundreds of "technically viable but economically irrelevant" networks, operating on inertia, gradually die out.

The Age of Specialization

Furthermore, the rise of second-layer networks in the Ethereum ecosystem and the consolidation of their dominant position have completely rewritten the "rules of the game" for infrastructure design.

AminCad, a core participant in the Ethereum ecosystem, pointed out that almost all "mainstream alternative first-layer networks with significant market capitalization" were launched before Ethereum's "DenCun Upgrade."

This upgrade significantly improved Ethereum's scalability and reduced transaction costs for second-layer solutions. He believes that this upgrade renders the so-called layer-one premium of these alternative chains completely invalid, making it "essentially a relic from the pre-Ethereum layer-two scalability era." AminCad stated: "Today, there is no reason to launch as an alternative layer-one network rather than a layer-two network with Ethereum as the settlement layer from a scalability perspective. Therefore, there is no evidence that newly launched chains can earn any premium by being a single-layer network." He also mentioned that layer-two blockchains using Ethereum as a long-term settlement layer have an operating cost that is approximately 99% lower than "standalone alternative layer-one networks."

At the same time, the market is "rewarding specialization rather than generalization."

Successful blockchains no longer position themselves as "universal platforms," ​​but rather as "digital economies focused on specific verticals."

For example, first-layer networks such as Plasma and TRON focus on "global stablecoin payments," providing instant transfers, extremely low fees, and full EVM compatibility. The competitive advantage of these chains is not "general throughput" but "occupying niche tracks". The core of their differentiation lies in "practicality and narrative" rather than simple "architecture". In contrast, Kadena has neither.

This shift marks the industry entering a "more mature stage":

from "technical vanity" to "economic gravity."

Therefore, chains that can survive the "coming wave of integration" must have the following qualities:

"sustained demand" to attract real users, stable transaction volume, and a value cycle that "can prove the value of its own block space."

Upcoming Integration

Kadena's failure foreshadows the future direction of cryptocurrency's "overbuilt infrastructure layer."The market cannot sustain the current situation of "hundreds of chains competing for the same pool of liquidity and developer resources."

In previous cycles, "frenzied capital" masked industry inefficiencies, and venture funds incubated dozens of first-layer network projects, assuming that each project would find a niche market.

However, “liquidity is not infinite,” and users always prefer “more convenient” options.

In the next few years, “integration” will replace “expansion”: some networks will merge or interoperate through “shared sorters” or “modular frameworks”;

Others will quietly fade into obscurity, leaving only traces in GitHub archives.

Only those networks with “clear vertical positioning” (such as games, social networking, real-world assets (RWA), and institutional finance) can survive as “independent ecosystems.” This logic is similar to that of the early Internet: dozens of protocols once competed for dominance, but in the end only a few protocols such as HTTP and DNS became "universal standards," and the rest were quietly eliminated. Today, the cryptocurrency industry is entering its own "elimination phase." For developers, this means fewer "vanity chains" and more "composable infrastructure" will be built on "proven ecosystems." For investors, this is a reminder that “laying out the first layer of the network” is no longer a “broad bet on innovation”, but a “selective bet on ‘network gravity’”. The core lies in the ability to “attract and retain capital” rather than simply “computing power”.

data.title: Looking at the three key factors driving BTC prices: Is October 11 a buying opportunity?
data.description:
Source: VanEck; Compiled by: Golden Finance

Like most emerging asset classes, allocating to Bitcoin is both an art and a science.

However, three measurable factors consistently explain the majority of BTC price movements over time: global liquidity, leverage, and on-chain activity. Together, these factors provide investors with a practical framework for sizing and timing digital asset investments.

1. Global Liquidity

BTC Price Highly Correlated with Global M2

BTC price has long been closely linked to the fluctuations in global money supply. Since 2014, Bitcoin has had a correlation of approximately 0.5 with the growth of global M2 (r² = 0.25), meaning that changes in fiat currency liquidity largely explain its long-term returns. While this relationship tends to weaken during short-term shocks (such as the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic, the 2024 election, or the 2025 "tariff tantrum"), the broader trend of monetary expansion continues to dominate Bitcoin's cycles. A multivariate regression of the supply of the five largest fiat currencies on Bitcoin's price shows that changes in M2 explain more than half of Bitcoin's variance over the past decade (r² = 0.54). Since 2013, liquidity in the five largest global currencies has roughly doubled, from $50 trillion to nearly $100 trillion, during which time the price of Bitcoin has increased more than 700-fold. Among currencies, Euro M2 money supply remains the strongest explanatory variable (r = 0.69, t = 10), highlighting Bitcoin's growing role as a neutral reserve asset in the context of synchronized currency depreciation. VanEck's historical macro view is consistent with this data. In March 2023, VanEck CEO Jan Van Eck stated on CNBC that as the Federal Reserve nears the end of its tightening policy, both gold and Bitcoin appear poised to enter a multi-year bull cycle. Within this framework, VanEck's thesis is that Bitcoin acts as "digital gold," benefiting from weak bank performance, imminent interest rate cuts, and renewed liquidity expansion. Regional market dynamics have shifted significantly over the past two years in terms of Bitcoin price discovery. As shown in the chart below, Bitcoin returns during Asian trading hours lagged those during Western trading hours earlier in this cycle and during the 2020-2022 cycle. Now, Asian trading hours are leading global returns. This year, we've observed that Asia led the recent late summer rally and is now also leading the recent decline. This rotation may reflect tightening liquidity in Asian markets as central banks in India and China are willing to sacrifice domestic monetary growth to defend their currencies. This pattern is consistent with Bitcoin's role as an "anti-money printing" asset in global liquidity cycles.

Annualized average hourly return of BTC trading session

2. Leverage

BTC price is closely correlated with BTC futures open interest (OI)

BTC futures open interest (OI) rose 2.5x year-over-year before the October 11th crash

As of early October 2025, futures leverage was close to 95%, and cash collateral for Bitcoin futures reached an all-time high of approximately $145 billion. Open interest peaked at $52 billion on October 6th, then fell back to $39 billion on October 10th after BTC dropped 20% within 8 hours, a reminder of how margin calls are cascaded through the system. Notably, leverage has never remained above 30% for more than 75 days, suggesting limited sustained risk appetite. As of mid-October, Bitcoin futures leverage was at the 61st percentile of its historical range over the past 5.25 years. Meanwhile, the composition of leverage is maturing. Growing participation from institutions, miners, and ETF market makers has shifted trading activity to regulated venues such as the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME), where longer-dated, hedging-oriented contracts dominate. Leverage remains a double-edged sword, amplifying drawdowns while also reflecting growing confidence in Bitcoin as a financial asset. Against the backdrop of the deleveraging event on October 11 and the price of Bitcoin reaching its lowest level relative to gold since October 2024, we believe the current market is a buying opportunity.

3. On-Chain Activity

1-Year Correlation between Blockchain Revenue and Token Price

While macro liquidity and market structure are indicators of Bitcoin's short-term cycles, and these indicators often influence risk appetite across the cryptocurrency market, on-chain fundamentals offer the clearest window into the actual adoption of cryptocurrencies. Across major networks, token prices show a significant statistical correlation with blockchain-generated revenue, demonstrating that usage and value are intertwined. Among the major blockchains, Solana's network revenue has the strongest correlation with token performance (r²=0.71, t²=30), while Binance Chain's BNB has the weakest correlation (r²=0.13). The causal relationship between the two is complex: higher token prices catalyze more revenue-generating user activity, while sustained fee revenue strengthens long-term valuations. As major blockchain application ecosystems mature, they provide new channels for activity, which can improve the relationship between price and on-chain revenue. As the newest of the six blockchains in the chart above, Sui's network growth in DeFi and consumer gaming/NFT applications over the past two years clearly demonstrates this relationship. For Bitcoin, on-chain metrics such as transaction volume and network fees remain less predictive of daily price movements than liquidity or leverage. However, they remain strong evidence of network health. We believe that Bitcoin (and, to a lesser extent, Ethereum) are relatively less disconnected from these fundamentals than other blockchain networks due to their increasing adoption as store-of-value monetary assets in off-chain treasuries and exchange-traded products (ETPs). While these factors favor BTC and ETH, the continued growth of on-chain revenue and user activity is the strongest evidence that a blockchain network's value proposition transcends speculation. Setting aside on-chain metrics, Bitcoin still resembles an "eight-year-old" on its adoption curve, as Jan van Eck described it in 2023, but its growing correlation with global liquidity and leverage suggests that BTC is evolving from a speculative asset to a macro hedge against fiat currency debasement. Today, BTC is more like a teenager; despite BTC's growth, we expect market sentiment to remain volatile in the coming years.

data.title: Hyperliquid Vault Strategy Analysis: From High-Frequency Alpha to Risk Management
data.description:
Author: Kevin, Movemaker Researcher; Source: X, @MovemakerCN The Vault ecosystem on the Hyperliquid platform provides investors with a unique window into observing and participating in on-chain derivatives strategies executed by professional managers. This article provides a systematic quantitative analysis and strategy analysis of the most prominent vaults within this ecosystem. Evaluation Framework and Data Methodology For an objective and multi-dimensional comparison, we selected five representative vaults on Hyperliquid that rank among the top in terms of scale under management and performance: AceVault, Growi HF, Systemic Strategies, Amber Ridge, and MC Recovery Fund. Our evaluation framework will focus on the following core metrics to build a complete profile of each treasury strategy:

  • Performance Indicators: Period Total Return (PNL), Number of Profitable Trades, Total Number of Trades, Win Rate, and Profit Factor.

  • Trading Efficiency Indicators: Average Per-Trade Profit and Loss, Average Per-Trade Profit, and Average Per-Trade Loss.

  • Risk management metrics: Maximum drawdown, standard deviation of single-trade profit and loss, and profit and loss volatility ratio (i.e., average profit and loss divided by standard deviation).

  • Strategy attribution metrics: Profit and loss contribution of each currency, and long/short position bias in a specific currency.

For data acquisition, we extracted the longest available historical trading data for each vault stored on Hyperliquid. It is important to note that due to the platform's data storage limitations, the historical data period for high-frequency trading (HFT) vaults is relatively short, and the analysis window we can access ranges from three days to two months. For strategies with lower trading frequency, however, we can observe longer historical performance.

AceVault Hyper01

Analysis Data Period: October 16, 2025 - October 20, 2025

1.1 Strategic Overview and Market Position

AceVault Hyper01 is not only the scale of assets under management (TVL) in the Hyperliquid ecosystem, This strategy is one of the largest cohorts, and its performance is equally impressive. As of October 20, 2025, the cohort's TVL reached $14.33 million. Since its inception in August 2025, the strategy has generated $1.29 million in profits, with an annualized rate of return (APR) of 127% over the past month, demonstrating strong and sustainable alpha generation.

1.2 Trading Behavior and Performance Quantification

During our four-day analysis period, the cohort recorded 19,338 position closes, providing a highly accurate sample for analyzing its strategy.

  • Core Performance Indicators:

    - Net Profit (Total PNL): +$103,110.82

    - Win Rate: 28%     

  - Profit Factor: 3.71

  • Profit and Loss Structure Analysis:

    - Average PNL: +$5.33     

    - Average Win: +$26.00     

    - Average Loss: $2.70

  •     - Maximum Drawdown: $791.20

        - Standard Deviation of PNL: 26.84

        - Avg. PNL / StdDev: 0.199

    1.3 Strategy Profile and Risk Attribution

    Strategy Profile: High-Frequency, Asymmetric, Systematic Short Selling

During this analysis period, the source of the strategy's profits is extremely clear:

  • Short positions: cumulative profit +$137,804

  • Long positions: cumulative loss $33,726

This shows that AceVault's entire net profit comes from its systematic shorting of 74 altcoins. The largest profit contribution came from the $FXS short position (+$34,579), while losses were concentrated in the $HYPE long position (-$16,100).

Risk Management: Extreme Loss Control

This strategy demonstrates textbook risk management. With a TVL of $14.33 million and a trading frequency of nearly 20,000, its maximum drawdown over four days was strictly controlled to $791.20, an impressive figure. This is highly consistent with the average per-trade loss of -$2.70, demonstrating the strategy's systematic and extremely strict built-in stop-loss mechanism.

1.4 Summary

AceVault Hyper01 is a highly systematic high-frequency strategy with clear logic, strict execution, and a strong execution strategy. Its core model involves holding long positions in a basket of mainstream assets (perhaps as a beta hedge or for long-term holdings) while systematically executing a high-frequency short strategy across the broader altcoin market. During the analyzed market cycles, the strategy's excess returns were entirely driven by its precise capture of altcoin declines. Its top-tier risk control system ensures that even when executing low-probability strategies, losses are strictly limited to a manageable range, resulting in healthy and robust overall profitability.

Growi HF

Analysis data period: August 7, 2025 - October 20, 2025

2.1 Strategy Overview and Market Position

Growi HF is a strategic treasury in the Hyperliquid ecosystem that has demonstrated strong growth momentum. As of October 20, 2025, its total locked-in value has reached $5.1 million. The vault has been operating since July 2024 and has a public track record of over a year, generating $1.05 million in cumulative profits. Its annualized return over the past month has reached 217%, demonstrating the strategy's exceptional alpha generation and strong profit potential. 2.2 Trading Behavior and Performance Quantification Our analysis is based on detailed trading data from the vault over the past two and a half months, during which 16,425 position closes were recorded, representing a high level of trading activity among similar vaults.

  • Core Performance Indicators:

    - Net Profit: +$901,094

    - Win Rate: 38%     

    - Profit and Loss Factor: 10.76

  • Profit and Loss Structure Analysis:

    - Average Single Profit and Loss: +$54.86

    - Average Single Profit: +$159.00

    - Average Single Loss: $9.00

  • Risk Indicator:

    - Maximum Drawdown: $16,919

    - Single Profit and Loss Standard Deviation: 1841.0

    - Profit and Loss Volatility Ratio: 0.030

2.3 Strategy Profile and Risk Attribution

Strategy Profile: An Extremely Asymmetric "Long Hunter" Similar to AceVault, Growi HF's profit model is also based on an asymmetric payoff structure, but its expression is even more extreme. A 38% win rate and a high profit/loss factor of 10.76 reveal the core of its strategy: using a small number of highly profitable trades to fully cover the majority of minimally losing trades.

The profit/loss structure (average profit of $159 vs. average loss of -$9) perfectly embodies this strategy. This is a classic "cut losses short, let profits run" trend-following strategy.

Profit Attribution: Systematic long bias and excellent asset selection. This strategy's trading behavior demonstrates a strong bullish bias. Directional P&L: The vast majority of the strategy's profits came from long positions (cumulative profit +$886,000), while the sole short exposure ($LTC) also contributed a small amount of profit (+$23,554). Currency P&L: The strategy's asset selection is exceptional. During the analysis period, it achieved zero net losses across all 20 assets traded. The largest contributor to profit came from its long position in $XRP (+$310,000), which was the core profit engine during this period. Risk Management: Combining Volatility Tolerance with Strict Stop-Losses: Growi HF's risk management model differs significantly from AceVault's. Its high standard deviation of 1841 per trade P&L indicates that the strategy does not attempt to smooth out individual trade P&L fluctuations, but rather is willing to tolerate significant profit variability in exchange for the opportunity to capture large profits ("home runs"). However, this high tolerance for upside volatility contrasts sharply with its extreme intolerance for downside risk. An average loss of -$9.00 per trade and a maximum drawdown of only $16,919 (extremely low relative to its TVL of $5.1 million and profits of $0.9 million) strongly demonstrate the effectiveness of its risk control mechanism, which systematically eliminates losses before they escalate. 2.4 Summary Growi HF is a highly asymmetric, long-driven trading strategy. Rather than pursuing a high win rate, it utilizes an extremely strict loss control system (average loss of only $9) to "hunt" for explosive bullish trends (average profit of $159). During the analyzed market period, this strategy demonstrated strong profitability and near-perfect asset selection (0 losing assets) in the long direction. The beauty of its risk model is that it successfully combines the "high volatility of a single PNL" with "systematic downside risk protection" to achieve extremely good risk-adjusted returns.

Systemic Strategies

Analysis Data Period: October 13, 2025 - October 20, 2025

3.1 Strategic Overview and Market Position

Systemic Strategies is a Hyperliquid This strategy vault in the ecosystem boasts significant assets under management and a long operating history. As of October 20, 2025, its total locked value was $4.3 million. The vault, operating since January 2025, boasts a track record exceeding nine months, generating $1.32 million in profits, demonstrating the model's historical effectiveness over a longer timeframe. However, its annualized rate of return (APR) over the past month is only 13%, suggesting a significant slowdown in the strategy's recent profitability. The following provides an in-depth analysis of the vault's 11,311 closed trades over the past week. The data clearly demonstrates that the strategy has encountered significant headwinds in the recent market environment, resulting in a significant performance drawdown.

  • Core Performance Indicators:

    - Net Profit: -$115,000

     - Win Rate: 22%    

     - Profit and Loss Factor: 0.56

  • Profit and loss structure analysis:

    - Average single profit and loss: -$10.22

    - Average single profit: +$61.00

    - Average single loss: $30.00

3.3 Strategy Profile and Risk Attribution

Strategy Profile: Failed Asymmetric Model The profit and loss factor of this vault is far below the break-even point of 1.0, which is a direct quantitative reflection of its net loss state during this period. However, a crucial detail lies within the strategy's profit and loss structure: the strategy's average profit (+$61) remains twice its average loss (-$30). This demonstrates that the strategy's inherent "asymmetric returns" logic (i.e., earning more on winning trades and limiting losses on losing trades) remains intact. Therefore, the significant losses during this period were not due to a failure in risk management (e.g., stop-loss orders), but rather to catastrophic misalignment in signal generation or market timing, resulting in an extremely low win rate of only 22%. In other words, the strategy's trading system generated numerous false signals during the week. While the cost of each false stop-loss order was manageable, the cumulative effect ultimately overwhelmed profits. Exposure and Performance Attribution: The strategy has been under significant pressure recently, as evidenced by its risk metrics: Exposure: The maximum drawdown during the analysis period reached $128,398. This value nearly equals or even exceeds the total losses for the week, indicating that the strategy experienced a sharp drawdown from which it has not recovered during the analysis period. Directional Attribution: Losses were broadly distributed across both long and short positions. Short positions accumulated losses of $94,800, while long positions accumulated losses of $23,953. This strongly suggests that the strategy's bullish and bearish signals were acting against market trends during the analysis period. Currency Attribution: Most of the 56 currencies traded experienced losses. The largest single-coin loss came from a long position in $PENDLE (-$22,000), while the largest single-coin gain came from a long position in $LDO (+$13,000), but the size of these individual gains was far insufficient to offset the broader losses.

3.4 Summary

Systemic Strategies' long-term profit record demonstrates the historical effectiveness of its model. However, the performance data from the past week provides a classic example of a strategy model's inability to adapt in specific market conditions. The strategy encountered challenges in both its long and short signals during the current market cycle, leading to a significant collapse in its win rate (rather than its profit-loss structure) and ultimately a significant drawdown. This performance can be considered a significant stress test for the strategy model, clearly revealing its vulnerability under specific market conditions.

Amber Ridge

Analysis Data Period: July 12, 2025 - October 20, 2025 (Full Strategy Lifecycle)

4.1 Strategy Overview and Market Position

Amber Ridge is a Hyperliquid This strategy vault in the ecosystem boasts a very clear and distinctive strategy profile. As of October 20, 2025, its total locked-in value is $2.5 million. Since its launch in July 2025, the vault has generated a cumulative profit of $390,000, with an annualized return of 88% over the past month, demonstrating significant profit potential. The following analysis is based on all 4,365 historical transactions since the vault's launch, providing a complete understanding of its strategy. Strategy Profile: A Clear "Long Mainstream, Short Alt" Structure. This strategy has a very clear profile, exhibiting a typical "Long Mainstream, Short Alt" hedging or relative value structure. Among the 28 assets traded, this strategy demonstrates exceptional discipline:

    - Long positions: Executed only on the three major assets: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and SOL.     

    - Short positions: Executed only on the remaining 25 altcoins.

4.3 Profit and Loss Structure Analysis

  • Core Performance Indicators:

    - Net Profit: +$390,000

     - Win Rate: 41%      

4.4 Risk Analysis: High Volatility and Large Drawdown

While this strategy achieves impressive returns, it also carries significant risk exposure.

  • Risk Exposure: Its historical maximum drawdown is a staggering $340,000.

  • Key Insight: This drawdown is nearly equivalent to the strategy's cumulative total profit ($390,000). This reveals the strategy's high volatility and fragility, and investors have historically faced the extreme risk of losing nearly all of their profits. Performance Volatility: The standard deviation of individual profit and loss trades is as high as 3639, while the profit-loss volatility ratio is only 0.024. This extremely low value statistically confirms that the strategy's profits do not come from a steady accumulation of small profits, but rather rely heavily on a few large winning trades to establish its success. 4.5 Profit Attribution Analysis: Directional Profit and Loss: The strategy's entire net profit comes from its long positions. Data shows that long positions have accumulated profits of $500,000, while short positions have accumulated losses of $110,000. This data clearly demonstrates that during the analyzed market period, the gains from long major cryptocurrencies far exceeded the losses from shorting altcoins. Currency P&L: High Concentration of Profits and Losses. This strategy's performance exhibits extreme concentration: - Maximum Profit: Derived from a long position in $ETH, contributing +$320,000 in profit. This single trade accounted for almost 82% of the total profit. - Maximum Loss: Derived from a short position in $PYTH, resulting in a $180,000 loss. Amber Ridge is a logically clear but highly risky "long mainstream, short altcoin" strategy. Its historical performance demonstrates the model's significant profitability during specific market cycles (i.e., when mainstream assets rise faster than altcoins). However, investors must be aware of its risk-return characteristics: Highly Concentrated Profits: The strategy's success relies almost entirely on a few "home run" trades (especially long $ETH). Significant Drawdown Potential: The strategy carries a significant potential drawdown risk that is almost equal to its total profit. This is a typical "high-risk, high-reward" strategy, whose performance is highly dependent on market beta and the manager's timing ability. It is only suitable for investors with a strong risk tolerance.

    MC Recovery Fund

    Analysis report analysis data period: August 10, 2025 - October 20, 2025 (complete strategy life cycle)

    5.1 Strategy Overview and Market Position

    MC Recovery Fund is a highly focused strategy vault in the Hyperliquid ecosystem. As of October 20, 2025, its total locked value was $2.42 million. Since its launch in August 2025, the vault has generated $450,000 in profits, with an annualized return of 56% over the past month, demonstrating robust returns. The following analysis is based on all 1,111 historical transactions of the vault since its launch. Its trading frequency is the lowest of the five vaults analyzed, clearly demonstrating its low-frequency, more selective trading style. The strategy is highly focused, operating only in four assets: Bitcoin, Ethereum, SOL, and HYPE. Strategy Style: Excellent Long-Short Profitability. The vault demonstrates exceptional alpha capture capabilities in both long and short positions. Specifically: - Executed both long and short trades on $BTC, both profitable. - Executed only long positions on $ETH and $HYPE, both profitable. - Executed only short positions on $SOL, both profitable. This demonstrates that the strategy doesn't simply follow market beta, but rather, based on its independent investment research framework, holds clear, pre-defined directional views on specific assets. 5.3 Profit and Loss Structure Analysis Core Performance Indicators - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - Net Profit: +$450,000 - P/L Factor: 43.1

    • P/L Structure Analysis: - Average P/L: +$404.00 - Average Win: +$862.00 - Average Loss: $18.00

    This strategy's win rate (48%) is close to breakeven, but its P/L Factor is a staggering 43.1. This exceptionally rare and impressive value, the cornerstone of the strategy's success, means its accumulated total profits exceed its accumulated total losses by more than 43 times. Its P/L structure is truly flawless: its average loss is tightly controlled at an astonishing -$18, while its average profit is a staggering +$862. 5.4 Risk Analysis: Extreme Risk Control This strategy's risk management capabilities are its most prominent strength. Risk Exposure: Its maximum historical drawdown is only $3,922. Compared to its cumulative profit of $450,000 and TVL of $2.42 million, this drawdown is extremely small and almost negligible. Volatility: While the standard deviation of individual gains and losses is high (2470), this is not due to a lack of risk control, but rather is driven entirely by a few large profitable trades. The extremely low average loss (-$18) and minimal maximum drawdown demonstrate that this strategy's performance on the risk side (i.e., losing trades) is extremely stable and consistent. 5.5 Profit Attribution Analysis Directional P&L: The strategy achieved substantial and balanced profits in both long and short positions. Cumulative profits on long positions reached $240,000, while those on short positions reached $210,000. This demonstrates that this is a mature, all-weather strategy that can adapt to diverse market conditions. Currency P&L: All four currencies traded were profitable. The long position in $HYPE contributed the largest single-currency profit (+$180,000), making it one of the strategy's core profit drivers. 5.6 Summary MC Recovery Fund is a textbook example of risk management. It does not pursue high-frequency trading or a high win rate, but instead achieves long-term, stable growth by capturing alpha through a near-perfect, highly asymmetric profit and loss structure. The core of its success lies in its highly disciplined system, which keeps the vast majority of losing trades within a tight, fixed range (average -$18), while allowing profitable positions to fully develop. This is a high-quality strategy with a high level of maturity and very low risk, suitable for investors seeking stable returns.

    Summary

    Through an in-depth quantitative analysis of Hyperliquid's five top vaults (AceVault, Growi HF, Systemic Strategies, Amber Ridge, and MC Recovery Fund), we are able to penetrate the surface of high APRs and total returns and gain insight into the core of their strategies: not all high returns are created equal. Our analysis reveals several key conclusions: Risk control, not win rate, is the cornerstone of top strategies: Contrary to conventional wisdom, the most successful vaults in this analysis do not rely on high win rates (AceVault 28%, Growi HF 38%, MC Recovery 48%). Instead, their success stems from a common, rigorously executed logic: an asymmetric profit and loss structure. A prime example of "asymmetric victory": MC Recovery Fund exemplifies this model. Its impressive profit/loss factor of 43.1 is the product of near-perfect risk management: the average loss per trade is only $18, while the average profit is a staggering $862. The same is true for Growi HF (a profit/loss factor of 10.76). This demonstrates that their profit models aren't based on "high wins" but rather on "suffering minimal losses while capturing significant returns on gains." Maximum drawdown is a "stress test" for strategies: Comparing the "Maximum Drawdown" and "Drawdown Percentage" columns in the chart clearly demonstrates the robustness of the strategies. - MC Recovery Fund (drawdown $3,922) and AceVault (drawdown $791) demonstrate textbook-level risk management, with their historical maximum drawdowns being almost negligible. In contrast, Amber Ridge's drawdown reached $340,000, representing 87% of its total profit, meaning investors experienced extreme volatility, with profits practically wiped out. Systemic Strategies' recent $128,000 drawdown also exposed the fragility of its model.

  • Alpha comes from diverse sources: successful strategies follow different paths. AceVault systematically shorts altcoins through high-frequency trading; Growi HF is an aggressive long-only hunter, capturing trends under strict risk management; and MC Recovery Fund demonstrates a sophisticated long-short balancing act, serving as an "all-weather" strategy. This demonstrates the depth of the Hyperliquid ecosystem, which allows for the coexistence of different types of alpha strategies.

    • For investors, evaluating a vault requires more than just the superficial APR. The true value of a strategy lies in its risk management capabilities, as revealed by its profit and loss factor and maximum drawdown. In the high-volatility, high-leverage arena of Hyperliquid, an asymmetric profit and loss structure is the key to achieving long-term profitability, and extreme risk control is the only path to victory.

data.title: Solana Abandons Its First Web3 Phone After 26 Months — Users Cry Foul Over Broken Promises
data.description:
Solana Cuts Off Saga Support After Two Years — Leaving Early Adopters in the Dark

Solana Mobile has officially left its loyal customers in the ditch after announcing that it will be ending all software and security support for its first Web3 smartphone just two years after launch.

The company made the announcement on its website, stating that the roughly 20,000 Saga units sold will no longer receive Android updates or security patches, marking an abrupt end to what was once billed as a revolutionary step in decentralized mobile computing.

Unacceptable move by @solanamobile. Ending security updates for Saga after less than 2 years makes it e-waste and kills any developer confidence.

For context: the iPhone 6s from 2015 still gets security patches in 2025. Why would anyone build for a platform that abandons its… pic.twitter.com/UYYfDRQZbo

— The City Keeps Building 🛠️ (@TheCity777) October 23, 2025

While the software updates and security patches are no longer available for Saga devices, the phone will remain functional but permanently frozen on Android 14, with its last update dating back to November 2024.

For its early adopters — many of whom paid nearly $1,000 for the device — the announcement stings. The Saga was marketed in April 2023, and its support is now ending after less than 26 months of existence.
